response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with 20+ years of experience spanning web, mobile, and some embedded work, but he misses nearly every hard requirement of this role. There is no indication of Japanese language ability (a native-level requirement), his C experience is brief and dated (a few months at National Instruments in 2007-2008), and the resume shows no Linux kernel source work, no L2/L3 networking or switch development, and no security-technology experience such as MACsec or 802.1X. His current focus on TypeScript/Node.js full-stack and AI tooling is far from embedded firmware for LAN switches.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with senior/staff-level roles and strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some low-level and systems exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, DLang low-latency services, Linux experience
Demonstrated ownership of full development lifecycles, including open-source projects and developer tooling
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a hard native-level requirement
Minimal and outdated C-for-UNIX experience; no Linux kernel investigation or modification background
No evidence of networking (L2/L3, switches, SNMP/NETCONF) or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) experience
